segments.text: and the peoples plot in vain?
The kings of the earth take their stand and the rulers gather together against the LORD and against his Anointed One.
Let us break their chains and throw off their fetters.
segments.speaker: Rebellious Kings

segments.text: The One enthroned in heaven laughs; the Lord scoffs at them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Then he rebukes them in his anger and terrifies them in his wrath.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I have installed my King on Zion, my holy mountain.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: upon…: Heb. upon Zion, the hill of my holiness

segments.text: I will proclaim the decree of the LORD: He said to me, 'You are my Son; today I have become your Father.'
segments.speaker: God's Anointed One

footnotes: the decree: or, for a decree

segments.text: Ask me, and I will make the nations your inheritance, the ends of the earth your possession.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You will rule them with an iron scepter; you will dash them to pieces like pottery.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Therefore, you kings, be wise; be warned, you rulers of the earth.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Serve the LORD with fear and rejoice with trembling.
segments.speaker: Psalmist

segments.text: Kiss the Son, lest he be angry and you be destroyed in your way, for his wrath can flare up in a moment. Blessed are all who take refuge in him.
segments.speaker: Psalmist
